summary: Iteration 2 — TerrainPRO multi-scope AI quote wizard. Backend: 16/16 pytest pass including 5 new tests for POST /api/quote/multi-generate (success path with slab + retaining_wall returns scope-grouped line_items via gpt-5.2, plus 4 422-validation cases). Existing /api/quote/generate, /api/waitlist, /api/waitlist/count all still green. Frontend Playwright on public URL verifies: Step 1 renders 16 job chips across 3 trades (Landscaping 7, Concreting 4, Earthmoving 5), wiz-next correctly disabled until name+address+>=1 chip, chips toggle yellow/black state; Step 2 shows measurements-<jobId> panels per selected job, slab area_sqm auto-computes to 60 from 10x6, wiz-next disabled until all required numerics>0 and selects chosen; Step 3 renders wiz-quote-readout with 6 line items per scope (slab, retaining_wall), Total Range $14,524 → $15,976, Labour/Materials/Contingency/GST stats, assumptions, next steps, and 'Start a new quote' button. Waitlist form testids intact and backend persistence verified.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: multi_generate_quote LLM call still has no explicit timeout; if upstream stalls the request hangs. Wrap chat.send_message in asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=60).

server.py MULTI_SYSTEM_PROMPT does not include the `scope` field as required in the JSON spec — it's documented in the example schema but not in the Rules block, which works in practice but a strict 'every line item MUST have scope=<job_type>' rule would harden the contract.

EstimatorWizard.jsx evalComputed uses Function(...) to evaluate a regex-sanitized expression. The current regex limits keys to numeric values and only the catalog-controlled `computed` strings are passed in, so this is safe — but if the catalog ever takes user-supplied math, this becomes an XSS vector. Consider a tiny purpose-built parser.

EstimatorWizard.jsx: when a chip is deselected, `measurements[jobId]` remains in state (only the panel hides). On re-select user sees prior values which is actually nice UX, but worth a comment in the code so future devs don't assume it's cleared.
QuoteResponse.scopes echoes the request scopes verbatim — fine for now, but consider stripping `measurements` from echo if responses ever get large.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run inserts 1 quote (single) + 1 quote (multi) into db.quotes, and 1 waitlist row in db.waitlist (idempotent on duplicate). Test emails use random uuid prefix. UI test added 1 multi-quote to db.quotes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable. AI multi-quote takes ~15-25s (AI_TIMEOUT=90 fixture). The Playwright text assertion for 'Total Range' must be case-insensitive — rendered text is uppercase via CSS (e.g., use total_html.lower()). Public URL: https://quick-quote-ai-2.preview.emergentagent.com. Verified scope-grouped line items: slab=6 items, retaining_wall=6 items, total range $14,524→$15,976 (real gpt-5.2 output).
